error_signature: (SSH) Channel open failure: Too many open files
title: MySQL node fails due to too many open files
symptom: The MySQL node returns an error stating '(SSH) Channel open failure: Too many open files' after a period of operation. This disrupts all jobs that connect to MySQL.
not_this_if: Error occurs immediately after starting n8n
Using a different database or connection method
root_cause: The error occurs because the system has reached the limit of open file descriptors, which is set too low. This can happen over time as more connections are opened without being properly closed, leading to resource exhaustion.
fix_steps: Check the current file descriptor limit using 'ulimit -n'.
Run 'sudo sysctl -p' to apply the new setting.
Edit /etc/security/limits.conf to set both soft and hard limits for nofile and nproc to 65535.
Log out and log back in to apply the new limits.
agent_instruction: Increase the file descriptor limits on the Ubuntu server and restart n8n.
